text_blocks.text: This view reveals a somewhat less than altruistic
Christian intent of the British Empire; however, it was
paradoxical from the beginning, as Simpson and most
other writers pointed out. The Ottomans were portrayed as
the slavery, but even as the American and British fleets
were striking at the Barbary pirates on behalf of
freedom, their countries were promulgating a vigorous
African slave trade of their own. Charles George Gordon
is known as the saint of all British colonial officers. A
dedicated Christian, he spent his time between assignments
living among the poor and donating his salary on their
behalf. He won Ottoman confidence as a junior officer in
the Crimean War. In his later career he became a high
official in the Ottoman Empire, working as Governor of
Egypt for the Ottoman khedive for the purpose of
conducting campaigns against slavery and slavery in
